LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALLAN SCHUMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. MR. SCHUMAN SATISFIED HIS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KEITH DUNCAN BY PRESENTING ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT MR. DUNCAN CANNOT PROVE ANY OF THOSE CLAIMS. THE DECLARATIONS OF JEFFREY GREENBERG AND MR. SCHUMAN DEMONSTRATE THAT MR. SCHUMAN ACTED COMPETENTLY, DID NOT BREACH A DUTY OF CARE OR HIS FIDUCIARY DUTY TO PLAINTIFF AND DID NOT ACT WITH UNDUE INFLUENCE. MR. DUNCAN FAILED TO SUBMIT AN OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, OR ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING ANY OF HIS CLAIMS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 =302/HK

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-05-2018 Vacated

Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Discovery Responses, And To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted; And Concurrently-Filed Related Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Allan Schuman's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Discovery Responses, And To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted; And Concurrently-Filed Related Pleadings

DENIED as moot. Judgment has been entered in favor of defendant Allan Schuman. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312 which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/CVA)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Allan Schuman Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Allan Schuman's motion for summary judgment is granted. Mr. Schuman satisfied his initial summary judgment burden as to all three causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Keith Duncan by presenting admissible evidence that Mr. Duncan cannot prove any of those claims. The declarations of Jeffrey Greenberg and Mr. Schuman demonstrate that Mr. Schuman acted competently, did not breach a duty of care or his fiduciary duty to plaintiff and did not act with undue influence. Mr. Duncan failed to submit an opposing separate statement, points and authorities, or admissible evidence that creates a triable issue of material fact regarding any of his claims. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 =302/hk

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Continued To Nov-26-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Off Calendar Jun-28-2018 Continued To Oct-11-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALLAN SCHUMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Continued to October 11, 2018 per the ex parte order filed June 26, 2018.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Allan Schuman Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Continued To Jun-28-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Keith Duncan 650-921-7799 Plaintiff in pro Per, accompanied by his siter Colleen Duncan.
Damon Thurston, Esq. 510-465-3922 of Rankin Sproat Mires Reynolds Shuey & Mintz for Defendant ALLAN SCHUMAN.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Plaintiff's sister requests for a stay due to brother's medical condition and speaks on behalf of the Plaintiff. Plaintiff presents documents to the Court and opposing Counsel. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ALLAN SCHUMAN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Continued to June 28, 2018 at 9:30 am. Plaintiff to file moving papers to formally request to continue the Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication by June 18, 2018. Defendant's response is due by June 25, 2018. Each side to provide the additional filings and serve them in a way that is received by the other side on the same day. Courtesy copies to the Court also on the same day.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
